commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Prey. Two years after this (Calmet) the Assyrians revolted, and seized Babylon. (Justin i.)
They slew the useless women: but Zopyrus betrayed the place to Darius, who hung 3,000 of the principal inhabitants, and demolished the walls. (Herodotus iii. 150.; Usher, year of the world 3489.)
